title: SafetyOn Method, Spel Class
description: Returns status of the Controller's safeguard input.
notes: Use the SafetyOn property to obtain the safeguard status when your application starts, then use the SafeguardOpen and SafeguardClose events to update the status.
see_also:
ErrorOn

EStopOn

PauseOn

SafetyOn Example

VB Example:

If m_spel.SafetyOn Then

  lblSafeguard.Text = "Safe guard is active"

Else

  lblSafeguard.Text = ""

EndIf

C# Example:

if (m_spel.SafetyOn)

    lblSafeguard.Text = "Safe guard is active";

else

    lblSafeguard.Text = "";
examples:
If m_spel.SafetyOn Then lblSafeguard.Text = "Safe guard is active"
Else lblSafeguard.Text = ""
EndIf
